title: Pneumonanthe ochroleuca
intro: Pneumonanthe ochroleuca, also known as the yellow marsh cinquefoil, is a perennial plant belonging to the family Rosaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which can aid in the treatment of respiratory issues, wounds, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Pneumonanthe ochroleuca include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and triterpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is often used to make remedies for coughs, colds, and digestive issues.
taxonomy: Pneumonanthe ochroleuca is a species of flowering plant in the family Gentianaceae. It is native to Europe and parts of Asia, and is often found growing in wetlands and along streams. The classification of Pneumonanthe ochroleuca has been subject to some revision over time, with some sources placing it in the genus Gentiana. However, it is now widely accepted as a distinct species within the genus Pneumonanthe. The plant is commonly known as the yellow pendent gentian.
